trigger_keywords: what is the generated import line for named imports with aliases
how does the system handle string quote style in generated imports
provide the template for named import generation
show me the output format for imports from local libraries
what specific import string does feature generate produce
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The generated line is 'import { spec1, spec2 as alias2 } from "namedLib";' and the string quote style logic implies double quotes ("") are used in generated output.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the same layout rules as the type declarations in the core parser block
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The constraint requires parsing 'export type' declarations into a 'typeAlias' node with an 'isExported' flag, treating them identically to other type definitions stored in the 'typeAliases' array.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature01_parse_source_overview.json.description: Parse a small mixed TypeScript source string and return the structured source model. The model groups top-level imports, exports, declarations, nested resources, and the flat list of referenced identifier names (usages). Each node carries its source character span (start/end).

input.source: import {foo} from 'bar'; class Foobar {}; const bar = new Foobar();

feature02_parse_imports.json.description: Parse the various import syntaxes into typed import nodes: bare string imports, named imports (with and without aliased members), namespace imports, equals/require external-module imports, default imports, mixed default+named imports, and multi-line imports. A malformed default specifier yields no import node.

feature03_parse_exports.json.description: Parse export statements into typed export nodes: re-export-all (export * from), named re-export (export { } from) including aliased members, export-assignment (export = X), and a default export (which surfaces as a default declaration).

input.source:
export * from './OtherFile';
export { Specifier, Specifier as Alias } from './AnotherFile';

feature04_parse_enums.json.description: Parse enum declarations, capturing the enum name, its member names in order, and whether it is exported.

feature05_parse_type_aliases.json.description: Parse type-alias declarations, capturing the alias name and whether it is exported.

feature06_parse_functions.json.description: Parse function declarations, capturing name, exported flag, declared return type (including a type-guard return such as "str is number", and undefined when omitted), parameters, and local variables declared inside the body.

feature07_parse_variables.json.description: Parse top-level variable statements (var/let/const), capturing each variable name, whether it is const, whether it is exported, and its declared type; multiple declarations in one statement produce multiple nodes.

feature08_parse_interfaces.json.description: Parse interface declarations, capturing name, exported flag, generic type parameters, property members (name and type) and method members (name and return type, undefined when omitted).

feature09_parse_classes.json.description: Parse class declarations, capturing name, exported flag, generic type parameters, the constructor, properties (including those introduced by constructor parameter modifiers), methods (with visibility, abstract flag, return type, parameters and local variables) and get/set accessors.

feature10_parse_modules_namespaces.json.description: Parse ambient module ("declare module 'X'") and namespace ("declare namespace X") blocks into nested sub-resources, each with their own declarations.

feature11_parse_usages_ts.json.description: Collect the referenced identifier names (usages) from a TypeScript source: decorators, typed/assigned class members, parameters and default-parameter values, return values, property access (only the leading identifier, not sub-properties), function calls, indexer access, variable assignments, nested identifiers, and globally used / extended class names.

feature12_parse_usages_tsx.json.description: Collect referenced identifier names from a TSX source, including JSX element/component names, and identifiers used inside JSX expression braces and inside callbacks such as map().

feature13_parse_javascript.json.description: Parse a plain JavaScript source (script kind JS): imports, exported ES6 classes with constructor and methods, and a default export.

feature14_parse_jsx.json.description: Parse a JavaScript-React source (script kind JSX): imports, an exported class whose method returns JSX, and a default export.

feature15_parse_generic_arrow.json.description: Parse a source where a generic arrow function is declared inside a method inside an exported class, capturing the full nested model.

cases.expected_output:
{
  "kind": "file",
  "imports": [],
  "exports": [],
  "declarations": [
    {
      "kind": "class",
      "name": "TestClass",
      "isExported": true,
      "accessors": [],
      "properties": [],
      "methods": [
        {
          "kind": "method",
          "name": "test",
          "isAbstract": false,
          "visibility": "public",
          "parameters": [],
          "variables": [
            {
              "kind": "variable",
              "name": "a",
              "isConst": false,
              "isExported": false,
              "start": 77,
              "end": 112
            },
            {
              "kind": "variable",
              "name": "b",
              "isConst": false,
              "isExported": false,
              "start": 96,
              "end": 109
            }
          ],
          "start": 41,
          "end": 130
        }
      ],
      "start": 0,
      "end": 144
    }
  ],
  "resources": [],
  "usages": [
    "a",
    "T",
    "b"
  ]
}

feature16_generate_imports.json.description: Render import model nodes back to TypeScript source. Covers external-module (equals/require) imports, bare string imports, namespace imports, and named imports (empty, single-line, multi-line wrapped past the threshold, default-only, default+named). The configured quote style and indent size are honored.

cases.expected_output: import { spec1, spec2 as alias2 } from 'namedLib';
import { spec1, spec2 as alias2 } from "namedLib";

feature17_generate_symbol.json.description: Render a symbol specifier to source: just the symbol name, or "name as alias" when an alias is present.

feature18_generate_method.json.description: Render a method declaration to source. Non-abstract methods get a body that throws a not-implemented error; abstract methods are rendered as a single signature line. Visibility (public/protected/private) and return type are honored.

feature19_generate_accessor.json.description: Render get/set accessor declarations to source. Non-abstract accessors get a throwing body; abstract accessors are a single signature line. Visibility is honored; getters render their return type when present, setters render a typed/untyped value parameter.

feature20_generate_property.json.description: Render a class property declaration to source as an indented, visibility-prefixed, typed field line.

feature21_generate_parameter.json.description: Render a parameter declaration to source: just the name when it has no type, or "name: type" when typed.

feature22_generate_variable.json.description: Render a variable declaration to source as "name: type".

feature23_generate_not_generatable.json.description: Attempting to render an element kind that has no generator yields a neutral error result identifying that the element is not generatable, instead of producing source.

feature24_build_index.json.description: Build a reverse declaration index from a set of source files, mapping each exported declaration name to the list of origins that provide it (kind, name, and the source path it is reachable from). Re-exports are resolved: "export * from" moves all declarations to the re-exporting origin, "export { } from" moves only the named ones, and aliased re-exports rename them at the new origin.

feature25_import_is_new.json.description: Report whether an import is considered "new" (i.e. has no concrete source span). An import is new when either its start or end position is missing; it is not new only when both are present. Applies to all import kinds.

feature26_normalize_filename.json.description: Normalize a file path for indexing by stripping a trailing source-file extension (.d.ts, .d.tsx, .ts, .tsx, .js, .jsx) while leaving the rest of the path intact.

feature27_normalize_path_uri.json.description: Normalize a path URI by URI-decoding it and removing a leading "file://" scheme prefix, returning the bare path.

_constraints.output_rule: Dispatch logic must branch based on exact 'action' value to select parser, generator, or adapter logic

_constraints.input_condition: Input action is 'parse' with source containing 'import {foo} from "bar"'
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain a single 'file' node with one 'namedImport' under 'imports'
A 'class' declaration must appear in 'declarations' with empty arrays for accessors/properties/methods
A 'variable' declaration must appear with `isConst` true, and 'usages' list must include 'bar' and 'Foobar'
Produced 'namedImport' nodes must include `stringImport` kind for bare imports

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains 'import { A, B as C } from "lib"'
_constraints.output_rule: Produces a 'namedImport' with aliases mapped to specifiers
Produces a 'namedImport' with empty specifiers but a populated `defaultAlias`
Produces a 'namespaceImport' node with the namespace name as the alias

_constraints.input_condition: Input action is 'parse' with source containing 'export * from "./OtherFile"'
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'file' node under 'exports' array must contain an 'allExport' node

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains 'export { Specifier, Specifier as Alias } from "./AnotherFile"'
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'file' node must contain a 'namedExport' with both specifiers, preserving alias mapping

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains 'export { Specifier, Specifier as Alias }...'
_constraints.output_rule: Duplicate specifiers with different aliases must be treated sequentially but without deduplication in the output data structure
Must produce a 'typeAlias' declaration with `isExported` set to true
'function' declaration must capture return type in 'type' field
Variables declared inside a function body must be grouped under 'variables' key within the function node
'variable' node must have `isConst` set to true and `isExported` set to false unless 'export' keyword is present
'variable' node must have `isExported` set to true
'interface' declaration must have non-empty 'properties' array containing 'property' nodes
'interface' declaration must have 'methods' array containing 'method' nodes with `isAbstract` true

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains 'class Foo { constructor(public p: string) { } }'
_constraints.output_rule: Constructor arguments with public modifiers must appear in 'properties' array with `public` visibility and also in 'param1' mapping in 'ctor.parameters'
'usages' list must not include the literal 'null' string

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains 'functionCall(MyProperty)' where MyProperty is a class instance
_constraints.output_rule: 'usages' list must NOT include 'MyProperty', as it is a static property access, not a usage of the property itself
'usages' list MUST include the element name 'myComponent'
'usages' list MUST include 'myFunc'

_constraints.input_condition: Input action is 'parse' with 'scriptKind: "JS"' and default export
_constraints.output_rule: 'declarations' array MUST contain a 'default' node with the name and `isExported: true`
Specifies that default imports in JS sources are modeled as named imports with limited specifiers

_constraints.input_condition: Input contains generic arrow syntax '<T>() => ...' inside a method
_constraints.output_rule: 'usages' list must include the type parameter name 'T'; 'parameters' inside the arrow function must be empty
Must output `import { specifier, specifier as alias } from "name";

_constraints: import { spec1, spec2 as alias2 } from "namedLib";

hide.replacement: a standard import line format and a local supported string quote style
exact_values_to_replace: import { spec1, spec2 as alias2 } from "namedLib";
